page: Microsoft Azure E16as_v6 is an Eav6-family server instance configured with 16 dedicated vCPUs and 128.0 GB of system memory. Powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture, this virtual machine operates at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz across 8 physical cores with 2 threads per core. The system is managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core, positioning it as a memory-optimized resource. There is no bundled local storage or GPU hardware included with this instance, though it is Premium Storage capable.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core capabilities. The instance achieves top-tier results in stress-ng single-core CPU performance and Geekbench text processing, alongside a strong rating in Geekbench single-core scores. Multi-core performance, including stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core metrics, falls within the average tier. Memory bandwidth tests show average performance for small-block cached reads and large-block writes, but reveal a performance tradeoff with weak throughput on large-block uncached read operations. Large-scale LLM inference tasks, such as prompt processing with a 70B model, perform poorly, reflecting the lack of dedicated hardware acceleration.

From a cost efficiency perspective, the E16as_v6 offers a dense memory allocation relative to its compute resources, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallel processing or local disk storage. The instance is suited for workloads such as database operations, memory-intensive web serving, text processing, and general-purpose applications requiring a high memory footprint.
description: Microsoft Azure E16as_v6 is an Eav6-family virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs and 128.0 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance provides a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or GPU resources. Benchmarks reveal top-tier single-core CPU and text processing performance, while multi-core metrics, Redis operations, and memory write bandwidth remain average. A notable performance tradeoff is its weak large-block uncached read memory bandwidth. This memory-optimized instance is suited for database operations, text processing, and memory-intensive general-purpose workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E16as_v6 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 16 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor, delivering strong single-core performance for database and text processing workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E16as_v6 is a memory-optimized instance with 16 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC CPU, suited for database and text processing.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processing and high memory density for database and text processing workloads.

128.0 GB of system memory providing a high 8.0 GB per core ratio
